review_notes: The control CM-3 (2) is applicable to Kubernetes as it involves configuration change management, which can be enforced through admission controllers, audit logging, and RBAC. The function names mostly follow the naming conventions but needed slight refinement for clarity. The functions accurately map to Kubernetes security controls, ensuring changes are validated, logged, and controlled.
naming_improvements: Consider using 'configuration_change' instead of 'change' for more specificity in function names
title: Configuration Change Control | Testing, Validation, and Documentation of Changes
step1_reasoning: The control CM-3 (2) focuses on ensuring that configuration changes are tested, validated, and documented. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to several areas: (1) Admission controllers can be used to validate changes before they are applied, ensuring they meet certain criteria. (2) Audit logging can capture configuration changes for documentation and review. (3) RBAC can enforce change control by limiting who can make changes to configurations, ensuring only authorized users can apply changes.
